JP EN KO

privacy policy

Privacy Policy

Shop Kloset Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "the Company") recognizes that personal information is an important asset of individuals. We shall appropriately acquire and use personal information, comply with laws and regulations regarding personal information, and establish the following personal information handling policy to prevent and correct unauthorized leakage. We will manage and operate this information with the utmost care. Please note that the collection of personal information by other business entities through links, etc., is not within the scope of this Privacy Policy.

Article 1 (Appropriate Acquisition and Purpose of Use)

The Company shall acquire personal information by lawful means for the following purposes:

  • (1) To provide each service.
  • (2) To verify the user's age and identity.
  • (3) To analyze user profiles, activities within each service, and preferences to suggest other users as compatible partners, and vice versa.
  • (4) To provide information on currently offered services or new services under consideration, and to conduct surveys.
  • (5) To research, record, and analyze marketing data.
  • (6) To distribute email magazines.
  • (7) To improve existing services and develop new services.
  • (8) To contact users.
  • (9) To verify identity and respond to inquiries from users.
  • (10) To conduct lotteries for campaigns and ship prizes or products.
  • (11) To distribute advertising information for which the Company or a third party acts as an advertiser.
  • (12) To create statistical data regarding the use of each service.
  • (13) To prevent use in a manner that violates the Terms of Use.
  • (14) For other purposes for which the user has given consent.

Article 2 (Proper Management and Protection)

Personal information shall be strictly managed, and appropriate preventive and corrective measures shall be taken to prevent unauthorized access, loss, destruction, falsification, or leakage.

Article 3 (Entrustment)

The Company may entrust all or part of the acquired personal information to subcontractors within the scope necessary to achieve the purpose of use. In such cases, the Company shall sufficiently examine the eligibility of the subcontractor, stipulate matters regarding confidentiality obligations in the contract, and exercise necessary and appropriate supervision over the subcontractor.

Article 4 (Provision to Third Parties)

The Company shall not disclose or provide data to third parties except in the following cases:

  • (1) When the user has given prior consent.
  • (2) When based on laws and regulations.
  • (3) When there is a violation of the Terms of Use and there are sufficient grounds to judge that personal information must be disclosed to protect the Company's rights, property, or services.
  • (4) When necessary for the protection of a person's life, body, or property, and it is difficult to obtain the user's consent.
  • (5) When particularly necessary for the improvement of public health or the promotion of the sound development of children, and it is difficult to obtain the user's consent.
  • (6) When it is necessary to cooperate with a national agency, a local government, or an entity entrusted by them in executing affairs prescribed by laws and regulations, and obtaining the user's consent may impede the execution of such affairs.
  • (7) In cases of merger, company split, business transfer, or other reasons involving the provision of personal information.

Article 5 (Joint Use)

The Company may share personal information with business partners in providing services. In such cases, the Company shall publicly announce the items of personal information to be shared, the scope of joint users, the purpose of use, and the name of the person responsible for management prior to the joint use.

Article 6 (Procedures for Disclosure, etc.)

The Company shall respond without delay to requests from users for notification of the purpose of use, disclosure, correction, addition or deletion of content, suspension of use, erasure, or suspension of provision to third parties (hereinafter "Disclosure, etc.") regarding their own personal information. However, this shall not apply if the Company is not obligated to disclose information under the Personal Information Protection Act or other laws. Requests for Disclosure, etc., must be made according to the Company's prescribed procedures. Please note that a fee (1,100 yen per request including tax, bank transfer fees to be borne by the user) will be charged for the disclosure of personal information.

Article 7 (Disclaimer)

The Company shall not be held responsible if a user reveals personal information to a third party using the functions of each service or other means, or if an individual is identified based on information disclosed by the user on each service.

Article 8 (Creation and Use of Statistical Data)

The Company may create statistical data processed so that individuals cannot be identified based on the acquired personal information. The Company may create and use such statistical data without any restrictions.

Article 9 (Acquisition and Use of Attribute Information and Action History)

The Company may acquire and use information that does not identify individuals, such as the following examples, to make the content of services and advertisements more suitable for users. Information may also be provided to advertisers and information providers for the same purpose. (Cookies, logs, accessed URLs, content, action history such as reference order, device information such as individual identification information, and attribute information such as user's gender, occupation, and age.)

Article 10 (Compliance with Laws and Norms, and Review)

The Company shall comply with Japanese laws and other norms applicable to the personal information it holds, and shall review and improve the contents of this Privacy Policy as appropriate.

Article 11 (Inquiry Contact)

The Company will strive to respond appropriately to inquiries regarding the handling of personal information. For inquiries regarding our handling of personal information, please contact the following:

Shop Kloset Inc.
Parkway Square' 1 2F, 1-16-7 Jinnan, Shibuya-ku, Tokyo 150-0041, Japan
info@shopkloset.com
